lead: A fuel shock tied to the Iran war pushed U.S. inflation to its biggest jump in nearly four years, jolting the economic outlook and adding political pressure in Washington. Meanwhile, tech news centered on security and strategy, from an attack linked to OpenAI's CEO to fresh scrutiny of Anthropic's new model, Xbox management criticism, and renewed talk of Apple's foldable iPhone.
summary_points: March consumer prices surged as gasoline and diesel jumped sharply, producing the strongest U.S. inflation increase in almost four years.
A suspect was arrested after allegedly attacking Sam Altman's home and threatening OpenAI's San Francisco headquarters, underscoring AI security risks.
Anthropic's Mythos drew concern from cybersecurity experts, highlighting growing unease over advanced AI systems and their possible misuse.
Apple's foldable iPhone rumors intensified as testing advanced, while a former Bethesda executive criticized Xbox's handling of the game business.
